summary: Acoustic gems, electric fireworks, legendary Cobham jam
blurb: The show shines with pristine acoustic numbers and a high‑energy electric second set. Highlights include a soaring Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance and an epic drums > space > Other One stretch featuring guest Billy Cobham. Jerry’s tone is beautiful throughout, and Brent adds sparkle on Dark Hollow.
review: On October 30, 1980 the Dead delivered a performance that still feels fresh in the vaults. The first set rolls through acoustic treasures—Deep Elem Blues, Dark Hollow (with Brent’s electric‑piano sparkle), “I’ve Been All Around This World” and a heartfelt Ripple—while the band effortlessly transitions into an electrified second set capped by Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance. The third set erupts with Shakedown Street into a legendary jam: drums, space and The Other One are supercharged by guest virtuoso Billy Cobham joining Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart, creating what reviewers call “three drummers swarming over the beast like salmon.” Jerry’s tone is described as beautiful and his solos on Dark Hollow, Ripple, and The Other One astonishing; Phil lays down a solid bass foundation that sneaks into Heaven Help The Fool and cues the band into the marathon jam. The soundboard capture (gd1980-10-30.sbd.bershaw.flac16) offers crystal‑clear clarity, while Jim Wise’s audience tape (the recommended recording) balances ambience with crisp detail, making this show a must‑listen for anyone chasing that 80s magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked positive; multiple reviewers repeatedly praise the acoustic/electric balance, the Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance pairing, and the guest Billy Cobham drum segment. No negative comments appear, indicating strong consensus of excellence across the show.

best_recording.reason: Clear, crisp audience tape that captures both acoustic nuance and the high‑energy Cobham jam with excellent balance.

key_highlights: Stunning acoustic set featuring Deep Elem Blues, Dark Hollow and Ripple.
Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance delivers a seamless, emotive transition.
Epic drums > space > Other One stretch with guest Billy Cobham adds historic firepower.
Soundboard capture provides crystal‑clear detail for the electric climax.
song_highlights: I've Been All Around This World

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is beautiful; his solos on Dark Hollow, Ripple and The Other One are astonishing, and his vocal on ‘I’ve Been All Around This World’ is singled out as amazing.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a solid bass foundation, sneaking melodic lines into Heaven Help The Fool and cueing the band smoothly into The Other One.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s electric‑piano solo on Dark Hollow adds sparkle to the acoustic set, enhancing the overall texture.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart delivers powerful percussion throughout, locking in with Bill and guest Cobham for a thunderous drum segment.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s steady grooves anchor the show; his interplay with Hart and Billy Cobham creates one of the most celebrated drumming passages of the era.

must_listen_sequences: I've Been All Around This World
